senses.definition: a belief or doctrine about earthquakes or about seismic activity; or a movement or action resembling an earthquake in influence or impact
examples: The scientist spoke of seismism as a metaphor for the cultural upheaval.
Her lecture emphasized seismism as a force that changes landscapes.

senses.definition: plural of seismism; multiple beliefs or doctrines about earthquakes or seismic activity; or multiple movements resembling earthquakes in impact
examples: The anthology collected several seismisms from different researchers.
Variations in seismisms reflected diverse interpretations of seismic data.

senses.definition: an instrument that records the timing of seismic events; a device combining seismograph and chronograph functions
examples: The seismochronograph logged the exact moment of each tremor.
Researchers used the seismochronograph to correlate quake events with time markers.

senses.definition: the record or graph produced by a seismograph showing seismic waves over time
examples: The seismogram clearly shows the arrival of the P-wave.
Scientists analyzed the seismograms from multiple stations.

senses.definition: plural of seismogram; multiple seismic records
examples: Seismograms from the network were compared to locate aftershocks.
The archive contains thousands of seismograms.

senses.definition: an instrument that records ground motions (earthquakes) as waves on a graph
examples: The seismograph detected the tremor early this morning.
They installed a new seismograph near the fault line.

senses.definition: a person who operates a seismograph or studies seismic data
examples: The seismographer monitored the station all night.
As a seismographer, she analyzes fault movements.

senses.definition: plural of seismographer; multiple operators or analysts of seismographs
examples: Seismographers across the region synchronized their readings.
The group of seismographers published a joint report.

senses.definition: relating to seismographs or the recording of seismic waves
examples: They used a seismographic instrument to measure the tremor.
Seismographic data helped map the fault line.

senses.definition: pertaining to seismography or seismic recording
examples: They compiled seismographical data from several stations.
Seismographical maps illustrate earthquake activity.

senses.definition: plural of seismography record; charts produced by seismographs
examples: The seismographies were archived for future study.
Researchers compared seismographies from different stations.

senses.definition: devices that measure and record ground motion during earthquakes
examples: A new seismograph was installed at the monitoring station.
Seismographs provide data to scientists studying earthquakes.

senses.definition: the science or practice of recording seismic waves; the use of seismographs
examples: Seismography has advanced with digital sensors.
Her work in seismography contributed to early warning systems.

senses.definition: informal or shorthand form related to seismo- concepts; may appear as abbreviated branding or shorthand in texts
examples: The glossary lists seismol as a shorthand entry.
In notes, they used seismol to mean seismology-related topics.

senses.definition: relating to seismology or seismic activity
examples: They studied seismologic patterns across the region.
Seismologic research aims to improve hazard assessments.

senses.definition: relating to seismology; concerned with seismic waves and earthquakes
examples: Seismological records helped identify fault lines.
A seismological survey was conducted after the quake.

senses.definition: in a way that relates to seismology or seismic activity
examples: The dataset was analyzed seismologically to determine wave speeds.
Seismologically speaking, the area is highly active.

senses.definition: plural of seismology; the science dealing with earthquakes and seismic waves
examples: Her interests cover several seismologies, including tectonics and wave propagation.
Different seismologies contribute to our understanding of the Earth's interior.

senses.definition: a scientist who studies earthquakes and seismic waves
examples: The seismologist explained the quake's origin.
She works as a seismologist at the national observatory.

senses.definition: plural of seismologist; multiple scientists who study earthquakes
examples: Seismologists gathered to discuss recent aftershocks.
The seminar featured several seismologists from abroad.

senses.definition: alternative term for a seismologist in some languages; used rarely in English
examples: The seismologue presented a new model of wave propagation.

senses.definition: the scientific study of earthquakes and the propagation of seismic waves
examples: Seismology helps scientists understand the Earth's interior.
They teach seismology at the university.

senses.definition: an instrument that measures and records ground motion during earthquakes
examples: The seismometer recorded the tiny tremor.
Meteorological data were synchronized with the seismometer readings.

senses.definition: plural of seismometer; multiple devices that measure ground motion
examples: Seismometers across the country were calibrated.
The network collects data from many seismometers.

senses.definition: related to or measuring seismic activity; using seismometers
examples: Seismometric data were used to map fault zones.
Seismometric readings indicated a low-level tremor.
